articles.description:
Outback Steakhouse welcomes the fall with several featured dishes and a pair of color-changing platypus-themed drinks.

The seasonal menu items include:
- Down Under Trio - A shareable platter Bloomin' Onion petals with signature sauce, new Fried Mozzarella Bloomerangs with marinara, and Aussie Cheese Fries with house-made ranch.
- Three Cheese Spinach Dip (limited time only) - Made with Mozzarella, Parmesan, cream cheese, and Alfredo sauce. It comes with tortilla chips for dipping.
- Filet with Roasted Garlic Butter - A barrel-cut filet topped with roasted garlic butter and paired with a choice of steakhouse potato and a side.
- Platypus Punch (pictured; limited time only)- Deep Eddy Grapefruit Vodka, mango, and Country Style Lemonade, served with a color-changing tea egg and a keepsake platypus.
- Puggle Splash (limited time only)- A non-alcoholic version of Platypus Punch made with mango lemonade and blue tea.
- Kangaroo Court (limited time only)- A Shiraz Cabernet Sauvignon blend created by winemakers Mollydooker.
Photo via Outback Steakhouse.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Outback Serves Up New Platypus-Themed Drinks and More

articles.description:
Hungry Howie's honors its Detroit roots (the pizza chain was founded in the Detroit suburbs in 1973) with the launch of its new Detroit-Style Pizza.

The new pizza features a rectangular pizza with a "thick, airy crust, crispy caramelized edges, cheese layered to the edge, and sauce added as the final topping."
Hungry Howie's Detroit-Style Pizza is available now at participating locations chain-wide. Each pizza includes eight slices, and prices start at $12.99 (it's $14.99 here in Los Angeles).
Photo via Hungry Howie's.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Hungry Howie's Introduces New Detroit-Style Pizza

articles.description:
The white chocolate version of a Crunch bar, Crunch White, is back in stores for a limited time after a 20-year hiatus.

Where the Crunch bar features a milk chocolate candy bar with crisped rice, the Crunch White replaces the milk chocolate with white chocolate.
The limited-time candy bar arrives in time for the 2025 Halloween season and can be found in 1.55-oz single-size bars as well as 2.7-oz Share packs. Crunch White bars are available now through December 2025 at retailers nationwide.
Photo via Ferrero.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: White Chocolate Crunch Bars are Back

articles.description:
Pizza Hut's Chicken Bacon Ranch Crafted Flatzz is a "hand-crafted" personal pizza topped with crispy-fried boneless chicken pieces, applewood-smoked bacon, Ultimate Ranch sauce, mozzarella, and a parmesan oregano seasoning.

I got one for $5 during lunch time (the price goes up to $6.99 after 5PM). I also reviewed the Nashville Hot Chicken one earlier.



The Crafted Flatzz is about twice the size of a Pizza Hut personal pan pizza, but a good deal thinner (and less greasy) with a hand-stretched oval crust. It was somewhat football-shaped, which is fitting for the season, I suppose. It was very crispy on the surface while maintaining a soft crumb inside.
The pizza was cut into eight two-bite (or one big bite) slices with one small popcorn chicken-esque crispy chicken piece per slice.



The mozzarella was nicely melted, but the underlying Ultimate Ranch just tasted like your run-of-the-mill ranch to me. Between the two, there was a moderate creaminess and a herby tang throughout the pizza. The parmesan oregano didn't make much of an impact.
The bacon was a little sparse here, and I could have used a little more to cut against the strong ranch flavor. I feel like it's one of those "your mileage may vary" things, though.

Overall, Pizza Hut's Chicken Bacon Ranch Crafted Flatzz is nice for the price and crust, but leans too heavily on ranch as its primary flavor driver. Typically, when something is called "chicken bacon ranch," it's mostly chicken, then bacon, then ranch. This was more like "ranch chicken bacon." I would definitely choose the Nashville Hot Chicken Crafted Flatzz over it.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Review: Pizza Hut - Chicken Bacon Ranch Crafted Flatzz

articles.description:
Cold Stone adopts a carnival theme this fall by launching new Churro ice cream and reintroducing Honey Cornbread with Blackberry Jam ice cream.


As is typical for the ice cream parlor chain, the two fall flavors are featured in two Creations, including:
- New Caramel Carnival Churro Creation (pictured) - Churro ice cream folded with Cinnamon Toast Crunch, caramel, and sugar crystals.
- Cornbread is My Jam Creation - Honey Cornbread with Blackberry Jam Ice Cream mixed with cornbread, honey, and blueberries.
Both 2025 fall ice cream flavors and their associated Creations are available now at all Cold Stone Creamery locations for a limited time through November 18, 2025.
Photo via Cold Stone Creamery.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Cold Stone Welcomes Carnival -Themed Flavors for Fall 2025

articles.description:
Papa Johns introduces the new Papa Dippa, which the pizza chain is calling the "first-ever pizza designed exclusively for dippin'. It's joined by three new dipping sauces: Roasted Parmesan, Garlic Hot Honey, and Creamy Garlic Pesto.

The Papa Dippa is a large, three-cheese (provolone, Fontina, and Asiago) pepperoni pizza with a flattened edge cut into 16 strips/sticks instead of slices. It comes with a "Garlic Sauce Flight," which includes the three new sauces as well as the chain's signature Special Garlic Sauce.
Here's how Papa Johns describes the new dipping sauces:


Roasted Garlic Parmesan - "A Caesar-style blend with roasted garlic and Parmesan cheese."
Garlic Hot Honey - "Sweet heat with garlic and chili flakes for an added kick.​"
Creamy Garlic Pesto - "Smooth yet textured with basil and Parmesan for a rich, herbaceous dip."


The Papa Dippa is available now for a limited time nationwide for $13.99 (may vary; it's $14.99 at my local Papa Johns here in Los Angeles).
Nutritional Info - Papa Johns Papa Dippa
Serving Size - 1 stick
Calories - 160
Fat - 6g (Saturated Fat - 3g)
Sodium - 340mg
Carbs - 18g (Sugar - 2g)
Protein - 6g
Photo via Papa Johns.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Papa Johns Debuts New Papa Dippa

articles.description:
Taco Bell updates its Freeze menu with the addition of the new Orange Vanilla Cream Soda Freeze, as well as a non-orange-flavored version, the Vanilla Cream Soda Freeze.

The Orange Vanilla Cream Soda Freeze features a sweet vanilla frozen, carbonated slushy base swirled with sweet vanilla creamer and tangy orange syrup. Pricing starts at $3.69 for a regular, $3.89 for a large. Regular Freezes of any flavor are also discounted to $1 during the chain's daily Happier Hour from 2 to 5PM.
The Vanilla Cream Soda Freeze is the same as the Orange Vanilla Cream Soda Freeze, minus the orange-flavored syrup. Pricing is the same as well.
Photo via Taco Bell.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Taco Bell Launches New Orange Vanilla Cream Soda Freeze

articles.description:
Popeyes partners with popular YouTube series Hot Ones to debut the new Popeyes x Hot Ones menu, featuring Hot Ones-inspired dishes with varying degrees of heat.

Available for a limited time at participating locations nationwide, the Popeyes x Hot Ones menu includes:


Sizzlin' Sriracha Dippers (Mild) - Pickled garlic sriracha-marinated Chicken Dippers, served with a Buttermilk Ranch dip cup and a Hot Ones Sriracha sachet.
Smokin' Rojo Sandwich (Medium)- A crispy-fried chicken filet topped with Hot Ones' Los Calientes Rojo spread and pickles, on a toasted brioche bun.
Darin' Dab Ghost Wings (Hot) - Your choice of bone-in or boneless wings dusted with a Ghost Pepper dry rub and served with a Hot Ones Last Dab Ranch dip cup.
The Last Dab (Extremely Hot) - A sachet of Hot Ones' spiciest, signature hot sauce. It's available a la carte for $1.


For those who want to do their own Hot Ones-type challenge, Popeyes has put together the Popeyes x Hot Ones bundle. It includes the Sizzlin' Sriracha Dippers, a Sriracha sachet, a Smokin' Rojo Sandwich, and Darin' Dab Ghost Wings (bone-in or boneless) with Last Dab Ranch for $16.99.
As part of the collaboration, the Popeyes x Hot Ones menu will be featured on an episode of the show, making Popeyes the first wing brand to be spotlighted on the show.
Photo via Popeyes.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: New Popeyes x Hot Ones Menu Arrives

articles.description:
Taco Bell looks into expanding its Energy Refresca lineup with a test of the new Electric Punch Energy Refresca and Berry Burst Energy Refresca for a limited time at participating locations in the San Francisco Bay area.

The Electric Punch Energy Refresca features a tropical punch-flavored, fizzy drink with green coffee extract, L-theanine, vitamin B12, and guarana for energy. It contains 160mg of caffeine.
The Berry Burst Energy Refresca is a berry-flavored, carbonated drink infused with green coffee extract, L-theanine, vitamin B12, and guarana. It contains 160mg of caffeine.

articles.description:
Wendy's is in the process of quietly switching from whole-leafed romaine lettuce to shredded iceberg lettuce for its burgers and chicken sandwiches. The switch began this summer at some locations and recently appeared here in Los Angeles.

The move was reportedly made to improve lettuce consistency and maintain a reliable supply. Where the romaine lettuce was hand-leafed at each location, the shredded iceberg lettuce arrives already prepared in bags, so there's a convenience factor as well.
The lettuce change does not affect the chain's salads, which, depending on the salad, are made with romaine and/or spring mix.
Personal preferences between shredded lettuce and leafed lettuce on a burger/sandwich can vary. Generally, leaf lettuce tends to wilt faster as there's typically more contact between the burger patty and the lettuce, while shredded lettuce occupies more volume, which provides more of a buffer against heat. However, shredded lettuce is also a lot messier as it falls out of your burger/sandwich easily. It's also cheaper.
Additionally, for anyone who was getting lettuce-wrapped burgers, that's no longer an option at locations that have made the switch.
Wendy's has not yet updated its website to reflect the change as of this writing (pictured). I recently got a burger from my local Wendy's and definitely noticed the shredded lettuce, but didn't note any mention of it during my visit.
Photo via Wendy's.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Wendy's Switching to Shredded Lettuce in Burgers
